message:
test(pager): tighten split-lock parallel-prepare WAL-delay budget

The split-lock parallel-prepare test was using a 5ms SlowWalBackend
io_delay and a 3x-serial upper bound for the wall clock. 5ms × 4
workers = 20ms serial budget, so the test passed any schedule under
15ms — including a completely-serial one.

Bumped io_delay to 20ms (serial budget 80ms, limit now 80ms), making
the assertion meaningful: unless the WAL I/O actually overlaps
across workers, the test fails. The comment was rewritten to reflect
the new shape — split-lock + group-commit batching should keep wall
time "well under the serial budget", which at 20ms-per-IO is
visibly a ~2-3× speedup on the slow backend.

No production code change; the SlowWalBackend is a test-only impl.
